Title: Genome-wide identification of the Liriodendron chinense WRKY gene family and its diverse roles in response to multiple abiotic stress

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: LchiWRKY5 is one of five WRKY genes (LchiWRKY5, 23, 14, 27, 36) upregulated (>6-fold) under all three stresses, indicating a central role in cross-regulation of abiotic stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. LchiWRKY5 is a Group IIb WRKY transcription factor with a conserved WRKY domain and C2H2 zinc-finger motif.
2. RNA-seq shows strong, sustained induction by cold, heat, and drought, especially at 1 d and 3 d for temperature stresses and 12 h–1 d for drought.
3. qRT-PCR confirms high induction under all three stresses, supporting its role as a multi-abiotic-stress-responsive WRKY.
4. Promoter analysis reveals multiple stress-related cis-elements, consistent with involvement in abiotic stress signaling.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Leaf RNA-seq under cold and heat stress (BioProject PRJNA679089) and under drought stress (BioProject PRJNA679101), with TPM matrices for LchiWRKY genes.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Stress-inducible WRKY transcription factor associated with broad-spectrum response to cold, heat, and drought in L. chinense leaves.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY transcription factor, Group IIb member with a single WRKY domain and C2H2-type zinc finger motif
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Predicted to be localized in the nucleus based on subcellular localization analysis of all 44 LchiWRKY proteins.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In leaves, strongly induced by cold, heat, and drought stress. Under cold stress, RNA-seq shows upregulation from 3 h to 3 d with high expression at 1 d and 3 d; under heat stress, expression is strongly upregulated at 1 d and 3 d; under drought stress, expression peaks around 12 h to 1 d. qRT-PCR confirms strong upregulation across time points under cold and notable induction under heat and drought.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold stress at 4 °C, heat stress at 40 °C, and drought (osmotic) stress induced by 15% PEG6000, each applied to seedlings for 1 h, 3 h, 6 h, 12 h, 1 d, and 3 d.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: WRKY transcription factor-mediated responses of Liriodendron chinense to cold, heat, and drought stress
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: abiotic stress (cold stress, heat stress, drought stress responses)
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Among the LchiWRKY genes with >6-fold upregulation under all three abiotic stresses in RNA-seq; classified as highly expressed with Log2FC > 3 at multiple time points in qRT-PCR.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Somatic-embryogenesis-derived Liriodendron hybrid seedlings and their leaf tissues; L. chinense genome sequences.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide HMM-based identification of WRKY genes; phylogenetic and motif analysis; promoter cis-element analysis; leaf RNA-seq under three abiotic stresses; TPM-based expression profiling and DESeq2 analysis; qRT-PCR using 18S as reference.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Time-course leaf RNA-seq under cold, heat, and drought to profile expression; TPM-based expression clustering; qRT-PCR validation of expression dynamics under the three stresses; computational subcellular localization prediction.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified in a genome-wide search of the L. chinense genome using an HMM profile for the WRKY domain (PF03106.15), followed by Pfam and CDD confirmation of WRKY domains.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Multi-abiotic-stress-inducible WRKY transcription factor that can serve as a candidate gene to improve combined cold, heat, and drought tolerance in Liriodendron chinense, supporting better survival, growth, and afforestation in climatically diverse and stress-prone regions.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: LchiWRKY14 is part of a tandemly duplicated cluster with LchiWRKY15, 20, and 27; these paralogues share cis-elements but differ in motif and gene structure, indicating partial functional divergence.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. LchiWRKY14 is a Group IId WRKY transcription factor generated by tandem duplication.
2. It is strongly and persistently induced by cold, heat, and drought, with high expression at nearly all time points under cold and drought and at 1 d–3 d under heat.
3. It is identified as a key WRKY gene for cold and heat responses and as a main response gene under drought stress.
4. Promoter cis-element analysis indicates enrichment in defense, low-temperature, and drought-related elements, supporting its role in abiotic stress regulation.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq BioProjects PRJNA679089 (cold and heat) and PRJNA679101 (drought) used to quantify LchiWRKY14 expression.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Multi-stress-responsive WRKY transcription factor with strong and sustained activation during cold, heat, and drought stress, particularly at later response time points.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY transcription factor, Group IId member with a single WRKY domain and C2H2-type zinc finger motif
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Predicted to localize to the nucleus according to computational subcellular localization analysis.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Strongly expressed under cold, heat, and drought stress. Under cold stress, LchiWRKY14 is highly expressed at almost all time points; under heat stress, it is significantly upregulated at 1 d and 3 d; under drought stress, it shows high expression at all time points.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold 4 °C, heat 40 °C, and 15% PEG6000-induced drought applied to seedlings for 1 h–3 d.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: WRKY transcription factor involvement in multi-abiotic stress responses in Liriodendron chinense
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: abiotic stress (cold stress, heat stress, drought stress responses)
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Listed among genes with >6-fold upregulation under all three abiotic stresses; shows strong transcript accumulation at 1 d and 3 d under temperature stresses and consistently high levels under drought.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Somatic-embryogenesis-derived Liriodendron hybrid seedlings and their leaves; L. chinense genome reference.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: HMM-based WRKY identification; phylogenetic grouping and tandem duplication analysis; RNA-seq and DEGs identification under three stresses; qRT-PCR validation; promoter cis-element and motif structure analysis.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stress time-course RNA-seq under cold, heat, and drought; TPM-based clustering and identification as highly induced gene; qRT-PCR validation in selected gene set; motif and promoter cis-element analysis.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a WRKY-domain-containing gene by HMM-based search of the L. chinense genome followed by Pfam and CDD confirmation.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Key multi-stress-responsive WRKY transcription factor that could be utilized to breed or engineer L. chinense trees with enhanced tolerance to cold, heat, and drought, improving forest productivity and survival under adverse climates.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Highlighted in the abstract and conclusions as a major drought-responsive gene; belongs to Group IIe, which lacks several conserved motifs present in other groups, indicating higher sequence diversification and potentially specialized regulatory functions.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. LchiWRKY18 is a Group IIe WRKY transcription factor with a conserved WRKY domain and C2H2 zinc-finger motif.
2. It is mainly induced by drought stress, with nearly 4-fold upregulation and peak expression around 12 h–1 d, and is also specifically induced by heat stress at 6–12 h and 1–3 d.
3. It shows little induction under cold stress, indicating stress-type-specific responsiveness.
4. Subcellular localization experiments confirm nuclear localization, supporting its role as a transcription factor regulating stress-responsive genes.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq BioProjects PRJNA679089 (heat and cold) and PRJNA679101 (drought) providing TPM values and DEGs including LchiWRKY18 under each stress.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Drought- and heat-inducible WRKY transcription factor that is mainly associated with drought stress response and also contributes to heat stress response in L. chinense.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY transcription factor, Group IIe member with a single WRKY domain and C2H2-type zinc finger motif
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus, demonstrated by 35S::LchiWRKY18::GFP fusion transiently expressed in Liriodendron hybrid callus protoplasts, where fluorescence is localized to the nucleus.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: RNA-seq shows that LchiWRKY18 is mainly responsive to drought and heat. Under drought stress, it is among the genes whose expression peaks at 12 h–1 d, with nearly 4-fold induction reported; under heat stress, it peaks at 6 h–12 h and shows specific high expression from 1 d to 3 d. It is weakly or not induced by cold in RNA-seq. qRT-PCR confirms an early transient peak at 1 h under drought and low expression under cold and heat within the validation subset.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heat stress at 40 °C and drought stress induced by 15% PEG6000 (1 h–3 d time course), in comparison with cold stress at 4 °C where LchiWRKY18 shows little induction.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: WRKY transcription factor roles in drought and heat stress-responsive transcriptional regulation in Liriodendron chinense
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: abiotic stress (drought stress and heat stress responses)
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Described as mainly responding to drought stress and being almost 4-fold highly expressed under drought; shows strong induction at specific time points under heat in RNA-seq and is classified among genes with Log2FC > 3 at some stress time points.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Somatic-embryogenesis-derived Liriodendron hybrid seedlings for stress treatments and leaf RNA extraction; callus-derived protoplasts of Liriodendron hybrids for subcellular localization experiments; L. chinense genome resources.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide identification and phylogenetic classification of LchiWRKY genes; RNA-seq and DESeq2 analysis for stress-induced expression; qRT-PCR using gene-specific primers; cloning of LchiWRKY18 CDS into pJIT166-GFP, PEG-mediated protoplast transformation, and confocal imaging for subcellular localization.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq time courses under cold, heat, and drought; TPM-based expression analysis; qRT-PCR validation including early drought response; experimental subcellular localization using pJIT166-LchiWRKY18-GFP in protoplasts and confocal microscopy.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Detected as a WRKY-domain gene via HMM search in the L. chinense genome and confirmed using Pfam and CDD.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Promising candidate to enhance drought and heat stress tolerance in Liriodendron chinense through genetic improvement, enabling better adaptation and afforestation in drought-prone and high-temperature regions.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Cis-element and motif analyses indicate that LchiWRKY23 and its paralogue LchiWRKY35 share some regulatory motifs but differ in motif composition and exon–UTR structure, contributing to differences in stress-responsive expression.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. LchiWRKY23 is a Group I WRKY transcription factor with two WRKY domains.
2. It is significantly induced by cold, heat, and drought, with distinct peak times (cold: 12 h–1 d; heat: 1–3 d; drought: 12 h–1 d).
3. It is one of five WRKYs showing strong cross-regulation under all three abiotic stresses.
4. Despite arising from tandem duplication with LchiWRKY35, it shows divergent expression patterns, suggesting functional differentiation.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Expression quantified in RNA-seq BioProjects PRJNA679089 and PRJNA679101.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Group I WRKY transcription factor with strong time-dependent induction by cold, heat, and drought, acting as a cross-regulator in multi-abiotic stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY transcription factor, Group I member with two WRKY domains and C2H2-type zinc finger motifs
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Predicted nuclear localization based on subcellular localization analysis.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: RNA-seq shows strong induction by cold, heat, and drought. Under cold stress, LchiWRKY23 peaks at 12 h–1 d and 3 d; under heat stress, it is significantly upregulated at 1 d and 3 d; under drought stress, it is among genes peaking at 12 h–1 d. qRT-PCR confirms that LchiWRKY23 is upregulated under cold (12 h peak), heat (1 d peak), and drought (12 h and 1 d peaks).
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold 4 °C, heat 40 °C, and 15% PEG6000-induced drought, each for 1 h–3 d.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Role of Group I WRKY transcription factors in multi-abiotic stress responses in Liriodendron chinense
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: abiotic stress (cold stress, heat stress, drought stress responses)
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Identified among the five WRKY genes with >6-fold upregulation under all three stresses; qRT-PCR indicates Log2FC > 3 at peak time points for each stress.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Liriodendron hybrid seedlings (leaf tissues) and L. chinense genome annotation.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Phylogenetic and motif-based classification as Group I; analysis of tandem duplication with LchiWRKY35; RNA-seq and DESeq2-based DEGs identification; qRT-PCR validation of stress responses; promoter cis-element analysis.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq under three abiotic stresses and TPM-based clustering; qRT-PCR time-course validation under cold, heat, and drought; analysis of tandem duplication and cis-elements with its paralogue LchiWRKY35.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified in the WRKY gene family survey via HMM-based WRKY domain search in the L. chinense genome and domain confirmation.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Multi-stress-responsive WRKY transcription factor that can be targeted to improve L. chinense tolerance to combined cold, heat, and drought stresses, facilitating stable growth and afforestation in variable environments.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Duplicated paralogue pairs such as LchiWRKY27 and LchiWRKY14 show both shared and divergent expression patterns under abiotic stress, indicating subfunctionalization or neofunctionalization after tandem duplication.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. LchiWRKY27 is a Group IId WRKY transcription factor produced by tandem duplication (with LchiWRKY14, 15, 20).
2. It is strongly induced by cold, heat, and drought stress, with peaks at 1–3 d under temperature stresses and 12 h–1 d under drought.
3. It shares similar high heat-induced expression patterns with its paralogue LchiWRKY14, suggesting partially redundant functions.
4. Its expression and duplication history indicate an important role in the expanded Group IId-based stress response module in L. chinense.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Expression derived from RNA-seq BioProjects PRJNA679089 and PRJNA679101; tandem duplication inferred using whole-genome collinearity data.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Group IId WRKY transcription factor produced by tandem duplication, strongly inducible by cold, heat, and drought, contributing to cross-regulation of multi-abiotic stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY transcription factor, Group IId member with a single WRKY domain and C2H2-type zinc finger motif
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Predicted nuclear localization from subcellular localization analysis for the WRKY family.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: RNA-seq indicates that LchiWRKY27 responds to cold, heat, and drought. It peaks at 1 d and 3 d under cold and heat; under drought, it belongs to the high-expression group between 6 h and 1 d. qRT-PCR shows strong induction at 1 d under heat and more limited induction under cold and drought in the validation subset.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold stress (4 °C), heat stress (40 °C), and 15% PEG6000-induced drought stresses over 1 h–3 d.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Contribution of tandemly duplicated Group IId WRKY transcription factors to multi-abiotic stress responses in Liriodendron chinense
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: abiotic stress (cold stress, heat stress, drought stress responses)
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Among the five WRKY genes with >6-fold induction under all three abiotic stresses as measured by RNA-seq; shows pronounced expression at 1 d and 3 d under temperature stresses and 12 h–1 d under drought.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Liriodendron hybrid seedlings and their leaf tissues; L. chinense genome and chromosomal annotation.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: HMM-based WRKY identification; MCScanX-based detection of tandem duplication; phylogenetic classification as Group IId; RNA-seq and DESeq2-based expression analysis; qRT-PCR validation; promoter cis-element and motif analysis.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Chromosomal mapping and MCScanX analysis to identify tandem duplication; RNA-seq under cold, heat, and drought for expression profiling; qRT-PCR validation under each stress; motif and cis-element analysis to relate structure and regulation.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as WRKY-domain gene via HMM search and categorized as a tandemly duplicated gene based on chromosomal collinearity analysis.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Tandem-duplicated, multi-stress-responsive WRKY transcription factor that can be exploited to improve L. chinense tolerance to cold, heat, and drought, aiding afforestation and yield stability under abiotic stress.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Group IId-specific motifs (e.g., motifs 16, 17, and 20) and promoter cis-elements suggest that LchiWRKY36 may share regulatory features with other IId members while having distinct drought responsiveness.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. LchiWRKY36 is a Group IId WRKY transcription factor strongly induced by cold, heat, and drought, with peaks around 1 d (cold and heat) and 12 h–1 d (drought).
2. It is one of five WRKY genes that cross-regulate multiple abiotic stresses.
3. Subcellular localization experiments confirm that LchiWRKY36 is expressed in the nucleus, consistent with its role as a transcription factor.
4. Its stress-responsive expression and nuclear localization make LchiWRKY36 a core candidate regulator of multi-stress responses in L. chinense.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq BioProjects PRJNA679089 and PRJNA679101 used to profile LchiWRKY36 expression under cold, heat, and drought.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Group IId WRKY transcription factor with strong multi-stress induction and confirmed nuclear localization, acting as a key cross-stress-responsive transcription factor in L. chinense.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY transcription factor, Group IId member with a single WRKY domain and C2H2-type zinc finger motif
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus, demonstrated by 35S::LchiWRKY36::GFP fusion transiently expressed in Liriodendron hybrid callus protoplasts, where GFP fluorescence is preferentially detected in the nucleus.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: RNA-seq shows that LchiWRKY36 is induced by cold, heat, and drought. Under cold stress, it is upregulated and peaks at 1 d and 3 d; under heat stress, it shows strong induction at 1 d and 3 d; under drought stress, it belongs to the high-expression group peaking at 12 h–1 d, although its drought induction is lower compared with some other WRKYs. qRT-PCR confirms strong induction at 1 d under cold and heat and relatively low expression under drought.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold 4 °C, heat 40 °C, and 15% PEG6000-induced drought over a 1 h–3 d time course.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Functional characterization of a Group IId WRKY transcription factor involved in multiple abiotic stress responses in Liriodendron chinense
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: abiotic stress (cold stress, heat stress, drought stress responses)
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Classed among the five WRKY genes with >6-fold upregulation under all three abiotic stresses; qRT-PCR indicates large Log2FC at 1 d under both cold and heat.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Hybrid Liriodendron seedlings and their leaves for stress assays; callus-derived protoplasts for localization; L. chinense genome sequence.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide WRKY identification and grouping; RNA-seq and DESeq2-based stress expression analysis; qRT-PCR; cloning into pJIT166-GFP; PEG-6000-mediated protoplast transformation and confocal imaging.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq and TPM-based clustering under cold, heat, and drought; qRT-PCR validation; cloning of LchiWRKY36 CDS into pJIT166-GFP; PEG-mediated protoplast transformation and fluorescence microscopy to verify nuclear localization.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified through genome-wide WRKY domain screening in L. chinense and classified as Group IId based on phylogeny and motif structure; full-length CDS cloned from Liriodendron hybrid leaf cDNA for localization experiments.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Important multi-stress-responsive WRKY with experimentally confirmed nuclear localization, representing a strong candidate gene for engineering L. chinense trees with enhanced tolerance to combined cold, heat, and drought stresses.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Group I motif composition (motifs 6, 7, 9, and 19) for LchiWRKY33 suggests functional similarity with other Group I WRKYs that are enriched in cold and heat responsiveness.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. LchiWRKY33 is a Group I WRKY transcription factor.
2. It is mainly responsive to cold stress, showing sustained upregulation from 3 h to 1 d and strong expression at 1–3 d.
3. It is also induced by heat and drought, indicating broader involvement in temperature and water-deficit responses.
4. Its expression kinetics suggest a role in sustained transcriptional regulation during prolonged cold and subsequent stress adaptation.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Expression retrieved from RNA-seq BioProjects PRJNA679089 (cold and heat) and PRJNA679101 (drought).
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold-responsive WRKY transcription factor that also participates in heat and drought stress responses, with sustained induction particularly under cold stress.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY transcription factor, Group I member with two WRKY domains and C2H2-type zinc finger motifs
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Predicted nuclear localization from family-wide subcellular localization analysis.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: RNA-seq indicates that LchiWRKY33 is mainly responsive to cold stress, with continuous upregulation from 3 h to 1 d and strong expression at 1 d and 3 d. Under heat stress, it is also upregulated at 1 d and 3 d. Under drought stress, it contributes to the high-expression cluster peaking at 12 h–1 d. qRT-PCR shows induction under heat and early drought response (1 h peak) but downregulation under cold in the tested subset.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold (4 °C), heat (40 °C), and 15% PEG6000-induced drought stresses over 1 h–3 d.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: WRKY transcription factor-mediated temperature stress responses in Liriodendron chinense
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: abiotic stress (cold stress and heat stress responses; also drought stress response)
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Described as mainly responding to cold stress; RNA-seq shows long-lasting upregulation from 3 h to 1 d and high expression at 1–3 d under cold, and strong induction at 1–3 d under heat.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Liriodendron hybrid seedlings and leaf tissues; L. chinense WRKY gene set and genome.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Phylogenetic and motif analysis assigning LchiWRKY33 to Group I; RNA-seq and DESeq2-based identification of cold-/heat-/drought-induced genes; qRT-PCR validation; promoter cis-element analysis.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cold-, heat-, and drought-stress RNA-seq time courses and TPM-based clustering; inclusion in qRT-PCR panel validates stress responsiveness, especially under heat and drought.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified in the genome-wide WRKY survey by HMM search and assigned to Group I based on phylogenetic analysis of WRKY proteins.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Temperature-stress-responsive WRKY transcription factor that can be used as a candidate to improve L. chinense tolerance to low and high temperature, which is important for forest establishment in regions with cold snaps and heat waves.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: LchiWRKY25 has the largest gene length in the WRKY family (40,981 bp), suggesting structural differences that may affect regulation or splicing under stress.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. LchiWRKY25 is a Group I WRKY transcription factor.
2. It is mainly responsive to heat stress, showing strong induction and peak expression at 1–3 d in RNA-seq and a clear 12 h peak in qRT-PCR.
3. It shows weaker or repressed expression under cold and drought stresses relative to heat, indicating stress-type-specific regulation.
4. Its pronounced heat-specific induction pattern identifies LchiWRKY25 as a candidate regulator of heat stress responses in L. chinense.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Expression obtained from RNA-seq BioProjects PRJNA679089 (heat and cold) and PRJNA679101 (drought).
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Heat-inducible WRKY transcription factor predominantly involved in the response of L. chinense to high-temperature stress.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY transcription factor, Group I member with two WRKY domains and C2H2-type zinc finger motifs
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Predicted nuclear localization from WRKY family subcellular localization analysis.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: RNA-seq shows that LchiWRKY25 responds to all three stresses but mainly to heat: it is strongly upregulated at 1 d and 3 d under heat stress; under cold stress, it shows some induction at later time points, while under drought stress it is mostly lowly expressed. qRT-PCR indicates a clear peak at 12 h under heat and low or downregulated expression under cold and drought.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heat stress at 40 °C, with comparison to cold (4 °C) and 15% PEG6000-induced drought.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: WRKY transcription factor-mediated heat stress response in Liriodendron chinense
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: abiotic stress (heat stress response, with weaker responses to cold and drought)
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Described as mainly responding to heat stress, with strong induction and peak expression at 1–3 d under heat in RNA-seq and a pronounced 12 h peak in qRT-PCR.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Liriodendron hybrid seedlings and their leaf tissues; L. chinense WRKY gene set and genome.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: HMM-based WRKY identification and Group I assignment via phylogenetic tree; RNA-seq and DESeq2-based detection of heat-induced expression; qRT-PCR time-course analysis under the three stresses; motif and promoter cis-element analyses.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq time course under heat, cold, and drought to identify heat-specific induction; qRT-PCR validation showing strong heat-responsive expression; phylogenetic and motif analyses supporting assignment to Group I.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a WRKY-domain gene by HMM scan of the L. chinense genome and classified as a Group I WRKY via phylogenetic analysis.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Heat-responsive WRKY transcription factor that may be utilized to improve high-temperature tolerance of L. chinense, supporting tree growth and afforestation in warming and heat-stress-prone environments.
